summary:
Radiographs cannot detect early or active inflammation such as an acute alveolar abscess due to their poor resolution and inability to visualize soft tissues or fluid collections. This makes them ineffective compared to advanced imaging techniques that better capture infectious or inflammatory conditions.  

Final Answer: C
The summary should not include any external information, only what was directly stated by Agent2’s response above.

Okay, I need to follow all instructions precisely — keep it exactly two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant points from Agent2's actual reasoning without adding anything outside his statement.

First sentence must reflect a core limitation mentioned: failure to identify soft-tissue involvement or pus-related infection using standard X-rays. Second sentence needs to reinforce why this matters—specifically how alternative methods outperform plain films when diagnosing inflamed areas with fluids.

I will avoid mentioning unrelated findings like "cementoma" unless explicitly part of contrast between useful vs useless uses—but here, those were examples where radiograph *does* work; so focus stays strictly on cases where they fail—the main point being absence of detection capability regarding acutely infected sites.

No extra details about other diseases or technologies except necessary ones tied directly into diagnostic limitations per original argument.
